calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-12-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo, via Zoom, for and with Cassandra Eitelbuss, Personal Representative.

The court and counsel discuss the status of the blocked account. Ms. Callejo reports that Chase Bank has been unwilling to provide documentation that the account is blocked and offers advocacy for the court to order the money placed in her IOLTA account instead as it will be convenient and less costly to access the funds to pay for expected for payment of tax preparation fees, taxes and other miscellaneous expenses of administration. The court offers its position regarding ordering that funds be deposited into blocked accounts for the benefit of the Estate.

After additional discussion, the matter is taken under submission.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Of Receipts
Off Calendar Apr-26-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo, via Zoom, for Cassandra Eitelbuss, Administrator.

Ms. Callejo reports that a Petition for Final Distribution has been filed with a hearing date of Aug-09-2022. The court and Ms. Callejo discuss the issue of the inability to obtain signed receipts from JPMorgan Chase but that she was able to receive confirmation from them that funds are held in a blocked account and plans to ask them for a letter confirming the preceding. The court suggests moving the funds to a different financial institution who would be willing to sign the receipts.

After additional discussion, the Status of Filing Receipts is taken off calendar. A Status of Proof of Deposit of Funds Into a Blocked Account is set for Aug-09-2022.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Kahler. Not reported.

Off Calendar Apr-05-2022 Continued To Apr-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo, via Zoom, for Cassandra Eitelbuss, Personal Representative.

Counsel reports that previously waived fees in the amount of $757.50 will be paid shortly and that four of five blocked accounts have been opened and hopes to have the receipts filed by next week.

After additional discussion, the matters on today's calendar are continued to Apr-26-2022 at 9:00 am. Ms. Callejo is advised that if the required documents are not filed by Apr-19-2022, she is to file a brief status report by the same date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2239 43rd Avenue, San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Feb-25-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo, via ZOOM, for Cassandra Eitelbuss, Personal Representative.

The sale was confirmed at $1,380,000 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). The court confirms that proceeds from the sale will be placed into insured blocked accounts to comply with FDIC regulations and therefore, an additional bond is not required.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Gomez. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE
Off Calendar Feb-15-2022 Continued To Feb-22-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Dec-08-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo, via ZOOM, for Cassandra Eitelbuss, Personal Representative; Amit Taneja, via ZOOM, Interested Party.

Ms. Callejo reports that a realtor has been hired to sell the real property and that the real property is currently being prepared to list for sale. Ms. Callejo also reports that a sibling is the lone additional beneficiary and believes that she can obtain a waiver of accounting from them and that the only asset of the estate is the real property. The court asks for clarification regarding whether the expenses related to the home have been paid. Ms. Callejo advises that the mortgage and property taxes are current and does not believe that there are any additional debts related to the home. The court advises that it will be taking the Status Petition under submission with the intent to grant an extension of administration to May-17-2022 and confirms that Mr. Taneja has no objections to the court's tentative ruling.

The Accounting Status Petition is submitted. A Status on Close of Estate and Status of Accounting are set for May-17-2022 at 9:00 am In Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Aug-26-2020 Continued To Sep-23-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Franchesca Callejo, Esq., counsel for and with Cassandra Eitelbuss, Administrator (Ms. Callejo appearing by video conferencing, Ms. Eitelbuss appearing by telephone).

Ms. Callejo provides a brief summary of the status of the estate whereupon the Court reminds counsel that these status hearings now require either the filing of a Petition to Approve Status of Administration or a Petition for Final Distribution.

The Court continues the matter to September 23, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. for the filing of either Petition. No appearance will be required if either Petition is filed by that date.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jun-07-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for Cassandra Eitelbuss.
Andrew Schultz for Maria Van Eyk (Guardian).
James Blackman former Counsel for Maria Van Eyk.

Ms. Callejo stated that Lawrence Siracusa has signed his declaration stating that he was not going to be available today and that on behalf of his Client he supports continued administration of the estate. Ms. Callejo also stated that the Minor along with the Guardian resides in the property, that it is their desire to remain living in the house until the end of the school year, that they have retained a Realtor, but the property is not yet ready to go on the market and that Mr. Siracusa supports a one year extension of administration. The Court noted that the Administrator is currently appointed with limited authority, that if they sell the property she will need to come in to seek full authority and that a separate petition is required for any disbursement of funds. Ms. Callejo stated that when the property gets sold, the creditors and liens will be paid, and that they will either post a bond or deposit the remaining proceeds from the sale into a blocked account. The Court granted the Petition for Approval of Report on Status of administration (subject to review of the order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-31-2017 Denied

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, LIMITED APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-01-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for and with Cassandra Eitelbuss.
Hannah Salassi for Judy Cissney (Petitioner).
Vivian Holley for and with Maria Van Eyk.
Raymond Denman III also present.

Ms. Holley stated that she waives notice. Ms. Salassi stated that the purpose of having a third party is because the property is going to be occupied for a while by Ms. Eitelbuss and other family members, and that her Client may have a claim by way of a Trust from her Grandfather. Ms. Callejo stated that her Client can work with her Counsel and abide by the rules, and that Ms. Fernan is not a professional fiduciary. Ms. Holley spoke of family dynamics, stated that they want some communication and requested mediation. Ms. Callejo stated that she sent a proposal that has not been responded to. The Court stated that Ms. Eitelbuss is the appropriate person, that she has priority, that she is the Decedent's daughter, and that the issue of mediation and other family matters are not before the Court. The Court granted Ms. Eitelbuss' Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). Bond in the amount of $10,000.00 is required.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
